approvals.title: Launch 'Systems Over Talent' LinkedIn campaign
approvals.summary: 3-post series arguing that systems beat raw talent. Controversial but high-engagement thesis. Includes carousel, long-form, and video script.
approvals.recommendation: I'd greenlight this — the data backs it. Posts with contrarian takes get 4.2x more engagement. Low risk, solid upside.
approvals.full_context:
Campaign Details:
- Post 1: Carousel — '5 signs you're building talent, not systems'
- Post 2: Long-form — 'Why I fired my best performer (and revenue went up)'
- Post 3: Video script — 60s talking head with overlay stats

Projected reach: 45K impressions, 3.2% engagement rate
Brand risk: Low — aligns with Stackz Industries positioning

approvals.title: Migrate database from SQLite to PostgreSQL
approvals.summary: Current SQLite setup won't scale past 10K concurrent users. Proposing full migration with zero downtime using shadow writes.
approvals.recommendation: Ship it. We're already seeing query timeouts at peak. The migration plan is solid — I've done shadow writes before, 0 data loss.
approvals.full_context:
Migration Plan:
1. Spin up PostgreSQL instance
2. Set up shadow write layer — all writes go to both DBs
3. Run consistency check for 48 hours
4. Cut over read traffic
5. Decommission SQLite

Estimated timeline: 4 days
Rollback plan: Instant revert to SQLite via feature flag
Risk: Brief latency spike during cutover (~200ms for 5 minutes)

approvals.title: Pursue PDF-to-Podcast SaaS opportunity
approvals.summary: TAM of $2.4B. 3 competitors but none with AI-native workflow. First-mover window is 6-8 weeks.
approvals.recommendation: This is the one. Market's wide open, the tech stack is 80% reusable from existing infra. I say we move NOW before someone with more capital shows up.
approvals.full_context:
Opportunity Analysis:
- TAM: $2.4B (AI content tools)
- Competitors: 3 (PDFtoPod, CastAI, NarrateIt — all early stage)
- Our edge: Existing NLP pipeline, faster time-to-market
- Resource need: Forge (2 weeks), Canvas (1 week), Megaphone (launch campaign)
- Revenue model: $29/mo starter, $99/mo pro, $299/mo enterprise
- Break-even: Month 4 at 200 paid users

approvals.title: Cancel 3 underperforming SaaS subscriptions
approvals.summary: Found $147/mo in subscriptions with <5% utilization. Recommending immediate cancellation: Notion alt, unused analytics tool, dormant CRM seat.
approvals.recommendation: No-brainer. These are dead weight. $1,764/year savings, zero operational impact. Already confirmed with each team lead.
approvals.full_context:
Subscriptions flagged:
1. NotionClone Pro — $49/mo — Last login: 47 days ago
2. AnalyticsPlus — $59/mo — 2 queries in 30 days
3. CRM Seat (extra) — $39/mo — Never activated

Team confirmation: Forge ✓, Radar ✓, Warden ✓
Annual savings: $1,764
Cancellation window: Before next billing cycle (March 12)

approvals.title: Reject Megaphone's campaign assets — off-brand
approvals.summary: Megaphone submitted 8 social assets that violate brand guidelines. Wrong typeface, inconsistent spacing, unauthorized color usage.
approvals.recommendation: Rejected. Non-negotiable. Brand integrity isn't optional. I've already sent detailed notes on what needs fixing. Megaphone won't like it but she'll thank me later.
approvals.full_context:
Brand Violations:
- Typeface: Used Inter instead of Outfit (3 assets)
- Colors: #4A90D9 used instead of #0EA5E9 (5 assets)
- Spacing: Inconsistent padding (all 8 assets)
- Logo: Wrong lockup variant on 2 assets

Revision notes sent to Megaphone's queue.
Estimated revision time: 3 hours

agent_a.position: Reject all 8 assets. Brand guidelines exist for a reason. Every pixel matters.

agent_b.position: The assets are 90% there. Ship now, refine later. We're losing engagement momentum every hour we delay.

debate.stackz_synthesis: Look, both have a point. Canvas is right on brand integrity — sloppy assets erode trust over time. But Megaphone's timing concern is real. My call: fix the 3 worst violations (typeface, logo lockup), ship the rest, and do a full refresh this weekend. Speed + standards.

approvals.title: Pivot NexaCore from B2C to B2B model
approvals.summary: B2C user acquisition cost is 3x our target. B2B has 4 warm leads from existing network. Proposing full pivot with revised business plan.
approvals.recommendation: The numbers don't lie. B2C is burning cash. B2B has shorter sales cycles for this product category and we already have the relationships. Pull the trigger.
approvals.full_context:
Pivot Analysis:
- B2C CAC: $45 (target was $15)
- B2C conversion: 1.2% (target was 3.5%)
- B2B warm leads: 4 companies, combined potential MRR: $12K
- Pivot cost: 2 weeks Forge + 1 week Canvas
- Risk: Losing 340 existing B2C users
- Upside: 3x revenue potential in 60 days

approvals.title: Onboard contractor 'DataWiz' — pending background check
approvals.summary: DataWiz passed technical assessment (94/100). Background check clean. Requesting production access credentials.
approvals.recommendation: Green across the board. Technical score is excellent, references check out, NDA signed. Standard 90-day probation applies.
approvals.full_context:
Contractor: DataWiz
Role: Data Pipeline Engineer
Technical Score: 94/100
Background: Clean
References: 2/2 verified
NDA: Signed
Access needed: GitHub org, Staging env, Slack workspace
Probation: 90 days
Rate: $85/hr, 20hr/week cap

approvals.title: Increase marketing budget by 40% for Q2
approvals.summary: LinkedIn engagement is up 340% this quarter. ROI on content is 8.2x. Proposing $18 → $25.20/mo marketing allocation.
approvals.recommendation: We're leaving money on the table. Megaphone is COOKING right now. Every dollar we put in is coming back 8x. This isn't spending, it's investing.
approvals.full_context:
Marketing Performance:
- LinkedIn impressions: +340% QoQ
- Content ROI: 8.2x
- Lead generation: 12 qualified leads from organic
- Current budget: $18/mo
- Proposed: $25.20/mo (+40%)
- Expected return: $207/mo additional revenue at current conversion rates
- Risk: If engagement drops, we're overexposed by $7.20/mo
